purpose: Keep AANA package surfaces separated before PyPI, npm, Hugging Face, or service publication.

surfaces.purpose: Python runtime SDK, CLI, local middleware helpers, audit utilities, and optional service/eval entrypoints.
surfaces.boundary: Importable Python runtime package plus CLI entrypoints. Heavy benchmark dependencies stay in optional extras and generated eval outputs stay out of the runtime package.

surfaces.purpose: Dependency-light TypeScript helpers and HTTP client for Agent Action Contract and middleware integrations.
surfaces.boundary: Standalone npm package under sdk/typescript. It must not depend on Python benchmark tooling or generated eval artifacts.

surfaces.purpose: Optional HTTP API service exposing /health, /pre-tool-check, /agent-check, token auth, OpenAPI docs, and redacted audit JSONL logging.
surfaces.boundary: HTTP service code and API examples only. It is installed through the Python api extra and must not become the benchmark runner.

surfaces.purpose: Benchmark, HF dataset, calibration, and evidence-pack tooling used for measurement and validation, not runtime core claims.
surfaces.boundary: Offline validation, calibration, and benchmark scripts. These artifacts support evidence and claims, but they are not required for core runtime use.

surfaces.purpose: Documentation, public claim boundaries, Hugging Face card templates, and publication guidance.
surfaces.boundary: Human-readable docs, cards, reports, and public claim guidance. Docs may link examples and evals but should not own executable demo code.

surfaces.purpose: Runnable quickstarts, integration demos, API fixtures, and Hugging Face Space demo source for developer adoption.
surfaces.boundary: Synthetic-only developer examples and demo fixtures. Examples must not contain real credentials, real sends/deletes/purchases/deploys/exports, or benchmark answer keys.

distribution_rename_plan.reason_to_consider_later: The public package now includes runtime SDK/API surfaces, so a future distribution name may need to be cleaner than aana-eval-pipeline.

required_checks: confirm distribution name and migration notes
confirm package owner and npm access
confirm no benchmark probe results are used as public claims
